verses.text: 'A good name is better than precious ointment'; better to visit the house of mourning than of mirth, for sorrow can better the heart. Death sobers the wise. To face our end teaches a seriousness that idle laughter never can.

'A good name is better than precious ointment'; better to visit the house of mourning than of mirth, for sorrow can better the heart. Death sobers the wise. To face our end teaches a seriousness that idle laughter never can.

'A good name is better than precious ointment'; better to visit the house of mourning than of mirth, for sorrow can better the heart. Death sobers the wise. To face our end teaches a seriousness that idle laughter never can.

Better to hear the wise man's rebuke than the song of fools, whose laughter is like crackling thorns; oppression and bribes corrupt even the wise. Honest correction is precious. Flattery and gain can unsettle the soundest mind.

Better to hear the wise man's rebuke than the song of fools, whose laughter is like crackling thorns; oppression and bribes corrupt even the wise. Honest correction is precious. Flattery and gain can unsettle the soundest mind.

'Better is the end of a thing than the beginning'; be patient, not proud or quickly angry, and do not pine that 'the former days were better.' Endings prove beginnings. Patience and contentment with the present are marks of wisdom.

'Better is the end of a thing than the beginning'; be patient, not proud or quickly angry, and do not pine that 'the former days were better.' Endings prove beginnings. Patience and contentment with the present are marks of wisdom.

'Better is the end of a thing than the beginning'; be patient, not proud or quickly angry, and do not pine that 'the former days were better.' Endings prove beginnings. Patience and contentment with the present are marks of wisdom.
Wisdom with an inheritance is good, a defence like money, but 'wisdom giveth life to them that have it.' Wisdom outlasts wealth. It preserves the soul where riches only shelter the body.
Wisdom with an inheritance is good, a defence like money, but 'wisdom giveth life to them that have it.' Wisdom outlasts wealth. It preserves the soul where riches only shelter the body.

Consider God's work, which none can straighten; 'in the day of prosperity be joyful, but in the day of adversity consider.' God sends both. Good days and hard days alike come from His hand, that we may walk humbly.

Consider God's work, which none can straighten; 'in the day of prosperity be joyful, but in the day of adversity consider.' God sends both. Good days and hard days alike come from His hand, that we may walk humbly.

Solomon saw the just perish and the wicked prosper; he warns against being 'righteous over much' or wicked, for 'he that feareth God shall come forth of them all.' Extremes are dangerous. The fear of God keeps a man balanced and safe.

Wisdom strengthens more than ten rulers, yet 'there is not a just man upon earth, that... sinneth not'; so heed not every word, for you too have cursed others. None is sinless. Knowing our own faults makes us slow to resent others'.

He sought wisdom but found its depths 'far off,' beyond finding out, as he applied his heart to know folly and madness. Full understanding eludes us. The wise admit how much of God's work lies beyond their reach.

He found the snaring woman 'more bitter than death,' from whom the godly escape; and concluded, 'God hath made man upright; but they have sought out many inventions.' Sin is man's own doing. God made us good; we have twisted ourselves with countless devices.
